channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Musk waves a chainsaw and charms conservatives talking up Trump's cost-cutting efforts
item.description: OXON HILL, Md. (AP) — Billionaire Elon Musk appeared at a conservative gathering outside Washington on Thursday waving a chainsaw in the air, showing openness to auditing the Federal Reserve and accusing Democrats of “treason.
item.dc:creator: ADRIANA GOMEZ LICON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump holds Black History Month event as some agencies skip recognition after anti-DEI order
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump marked Black History Month at the White House on Thursday by making an appearance at a celebratory reception with a surprise guest, golf legend Tiger Woods, while calling out other athletes in attendance and marveling at the size of the crowd.
item.dc:creator: MATT BROWN and MICHELLE L. PRICE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Senate ready to stay up all night to pass GOP budget over objections from Democrats
item.description: Senators are launching a budget “vote-a-rama” in a crucial, if dreaded, step toward President Donald Trump's agenda.
item.dc:creator: Lisa Mascaro And Kevin Freking, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: New York governor won't remove NYC mayor, for now, but plans to increase oversight of City Hall
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Gov. Kathy Hochul won't immediately remove New York City Mayor Eric Adams from office, but will instead push for increased oversight of City Hall as he faces intense scrutiny over his bribery case and his relationship with the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: ANTHONY IZAGUIRRE, JAKE OFFENHARTZ and JENNIFER PELTZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Federal department cafeteria empty for years under Biden: source
item.description: The Department of Interior (DOI) cafeteria remained closed for years under the Biden administration, Fox News Digital has learned.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Walz family starts to make a move
item.description: After a nearly two-year renovation of the governor’s residence, the Summit Avenue mansion is ready to serve as the first family’s living quarters again.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson, MPR News

item.title: EXCLUSIVE: Dems to force votes on Medicaid as it becomes sore point in Senate budget fight
item.description: Democrats are planning to make Republicans take uncomfortable amendment votes, including several on protecting Medicaid from cuts.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: USDA to follow ‘president’s lead’ on foreign farm ownership policy
item.description: The concern, however, is that international ownership could lead to risks of spying or economic pressure.
item.dc:creator: Alex Caprariello, NewsNation

item.title: Once off the table, bills to charge women who get abortions with murder get votes before failing
item.description: Abortion rights advocates feared the 2022 U.S. Supreme Court ruling that opened the door to state abortion bans would also lead to tracking women and charging women who get abortions with murder.
item.dc:creator: KIMBERLEE KRUESI and GEOFF MULVIHILL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Texas House wants to give public schools $220 more per student
item.description: The proposal, filed Thursday, comes well short of the increase school administrators and education advocates have been asking for.
item.dc:creator: Texas Tribune, By Pavan Acharya, KPRC 2

item.title: Elon Musk's SpaceX engineers deployed to FAA to help modernize air traffic control
item.description: Secretary of Transportation Sean Duffy said Elon Musk's SpaceX engineers would make recommendations to the FAA on how to upgrade air traffic control systems.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump’s vow to preserve Medicaid collides with House GOP plan for tax cuts
item.description: President Trump’s vow to keep Medicaid intact is colliding headlong into the Republicans’ strategy to extend their 2017 tax cuts, complicating the GOP effort to secure a major policy win in the early months of Trump’s second term.
item.dc:creator: Mike Lillis, NewsNation

item.title: Senate committee advances ex-wrestling CEO Linda McMahon as Trump's nominee for education secretary
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A Senate committee voted Thursday to advance Linda McMahon's nomination to serve as President Donald Trump's education secretary, bringing her closer to leading an agency the Republican president wants to shut down.
item.dc:creator: COLLIN BINKLEY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Sen. Mitch McConnell won't seek reelection in 2026, ending long tenure as Republican power broker
item.description: Republican Sen. Mitch McConnell has announced that he won’t seek reelection next year, ending a decadeslong tenure as a power broker who championed conservative causes but ultimately ceded ground to the fierce GOP populism of President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: Bruce Schreiner, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump Education pick Linda McMahon clears committee, heads to full Senate
item.description: Linda McMahon, President Trump’s pick for Education secretary, passed the Senate Health, Education, Labor, and Pensions (HELP) Committee on Thursday morning, sending her confirmation to the full Senate.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, NewsNation

item.title: A gold-framed Trump mug shot is hanging just outside the Oval Office
item.description: President Trump’s mug shot is one of the first things visitors will see when approaching the Oval Office.
item.dc:creator: Alana Wise, MPR News

item.title: Justice Department official urges quick dismissal of case against NYC mayor
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — In an extraordinary courtroom showdown, a federal judge pressed the Justice Department on Wednesday on its demand to throw out New York City Mayor Eric Adams’ corruption case, grilling a top official and putting the Democratic mayor under oath to answer concerns that the Trump admini
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Latino business owners say customers are staying away in wake of deportation threats
item.description: Some Latino business owners say customers have been staying away out of fear of being detained and deported. Twin Cities business and city leaders are encouraging people to buy from Latino-owned shops this Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Regina Medina, MPR News

item.title: Florida GOP pushes for government efficiency with DOGE-inspired legislation
item.description: President Donald Trump's Department of Government Efficiency, also known as DOGE, has stirred up controversy as it searches for wasteful federal spending. Now, Florida GOP lawmakers are planning a similar initiative.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Florida Democrats file bill to repeal 6-week abortion ban
item.description: House Minority Leader Fentrice Driskell, who represents families in Hillsborough County, said the legislation comes because it’s a mandate from voters.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: DHS fires roughly 400 probationary employees
item.description: The firings came after the Office of Personnel Management directed agencies to fire probationary employees, typically those who have been on the job for up to one year.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, Fox 5

item.title: Politicians demand cuts to 9/11 health program be reversed
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – The 9/11 health program is taking a heavy hit from Elon Musk's Department of Government Efficiency. Twenty percent of the program's staff were just fired or took buyouts. Already strained workers will now be slower in processing the sick, with far greater wait times and delays.
item.dc:creator: John Muller, PIX 11

item.title: Gov. Gavin Newsom proposes $125M mortgage relief program to help victims of recent natural disasters
item.description: Gov. Gavin Newsom on Wednesday proposed a new $125 million package to help residents whose homes were destroyed or damaged by recent natural disasters, including the recent wildfires.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, Fox 5

item.title: Minnesota bill seeks to disable AI tech used to create nude or sexual images of people
item.description: The plan would require artificial intelligence companies to deactivate a feature that allows users to make people appear naked or engaged in sexual acts in photos or videos without their consent.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson, Nina Moini, and Alanna Elder, MPR News

item.title: DOGE affiliate may access IRS taxpayer records: Report
item.description: An IRS employee with affiliations to the Department of Government Efficiency may be able to gain access to IRS taxpayer records.
item.dc:creator: Jordan Perkins, NewsNation

item.title: Jan. 6 pardon recipients argue it stretches to other crimes: report
item.description: Many defendants are arguing that crimes that were found during the riot investigations should be pardoned.
item.dc:creator: Sierra Campbell, NewsNation

item.title: Minnesota business leader among thousands of federal workers axed in Trump cuts
item.description: Thousands of probationary employees who have not been in their jobs long enough to qualify for certain protections have been terminated by federal agencies around the country, including in Minnesota.
item.dc:creator: Nina Moini and Ellie Roth, MPR News

item.title: DOJ and NYC mayor face judge's scrutiny in bid to dismiss criminal charges
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Justice Department lawyers and New York City Mayor Eric Adams are set to face a federal judge who is signaling that he’s unlikely to rubber stamp their request to drop the mayor's corruption charges weeks before an April trial. Judge Dale E.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: A federal judge has denied states‘ bid to halt DOGE and Musk‘s work
item.description: A federal judge has denied a bid to temporarily halt DOGE from its controversial work at certain federal agencies. Meanwhile, the White House says Elon Musk is not technically DOGE’s leader.
item.dc:creator: Stephen Fowler, MPR News
